project.name: OpenKrakey
project.description: Minimalist microkernel autonomous-agent framework (TypeScript/Node). Per-Agent: clock + event-system + orchestrator(+context-buffer) + loader, wrapped by agent_instance. Global: boot + cli.

core.description: The whole framework — per-Agent runtime modules + their L1 contracts + shared modules + the two global modules (boot, cli) + the LLM communication gateway.

plugins.description: Phase-1 MVP plugins under public_plugin/ — the essential set that makes a bare agent converse, remember, and use tools, exercising every core function. Each implements the L1 plugin contract and talks ONLY via the per-Agent event-system.

clock.description: Per-Agent dumb timer: countdown + activate; rhythm controllable.

event-system.description: Per-Agent independent central bus: eventbus + actionbus; clock/loader/orchestrator/plugins all connect.

orchestrator.description: Per-Agent conductor; CONTAINS the context-buffer. Event-driven beat: gather→compose→llm.request; dispatch tool calls from llm.return.

loader.description: Per-Agent plugin lifecycle: load private(override)+public (independents are no-copy: code from public_plugin/, dataDir redirected agent-private), build v1.1 PluginContext (leveled log + print + log.entry push), inject llm library, register (setup) into event-system, teardown.

agent_instance.description: Wraps ONE Agent: holds+wires clock+event-system+orchestrator+loader; receives the global llm library; start()->loader loads->orchestrator conducts; stop().

boot.description: Global startup ONLY: read each agent's config + config/llm.json, build the global llm library, build + launch each agent_instance. Composition root.

cli.description: Global standalone interactive (@inquirer/prompts) config tool: arrow-key pages to edit agent configs, the Default Setting, and the LLM communicator catalogue; Krakey logo; krakey/krakey agent/krakey default deep-links.

llm-gateway.description: Global LLM communication module: builds a key-less CommunicatorLibrary from config/llm.json (provider adapters: anthropic, openai-compatible; native fetch). Holds keys; does request+parse. Industry-standard infra, solidified into core.

llm-core.description: The LLM round-trip plugin: llm.request -> communicator chat (key-less ctx.llm) -> llm.return (+ output.message for non-empty content). Provides the llm.register_tool action other plugins call to declare ToolDefs.

persona.description: Stable identity context block (priority 10000+, stable-prefix convention) rendered from the agent's config.

web.description: Browser chat channel: a refcounted module-level node:http server (one per process); POST /api/agents/:id/message -> input.message + clock.fire_now; SSE /api/agents/:id/stream streams that agent's output.message; per-message sent/read status (read on llm.return). Serves a dependency-free static chat page.

notes.description: Persistent notes store (the roadmap 'library' demo made useful): note.save/note.read/note.list actions over dataDir files, registered as LLM tools; shared when public, agent-isolated when independent.

toolbox.description: Basic tools: time.now action + ToolDefs exposing the orchestrator's clock.set_interval / clock.set_default_interval rhythm actions so the LLM can self-pace.

inspector.description: Read-only browser debug/analysis dashboard: own refcounted node:http hub (loopback + token) + per-agent SSE; subscribes to ALL well-known bus events into a bounded in-memory record ring (live + scrollback); serves a dependency-free page showing prompts sent (llm.request composed context) / received (llm.return), the event timeline, and detailed logs (incl. core:* lines), correlated per-beat by request id. Emits nothing — never perturbs the observed agent.

config.description: OpenKrakeyConfig / DefaultAgentSetting / LLMConfig types + path constants.

theme.description: Krakey brand primitives (mint/dim/bold/red painters, ✦ glyph, ✔/✖ verdict lines) shared by every console surface (cli, boot startup report); degrades plain on non-TTY/NO_COLOR.

http-auth.description: Per-process session-token primitives shared by the loopback HTTP plugins (web, inspector): constant-time tokenOk (openWhenUnset knob) + Bearer/query/cookie extractors (cookie decode knob, fail-closed). Plugins keep their own source precedence + token-from-config policy.
